explanation: The LTL property captures the requirement by stating that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode, then whenever the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause) shall start in the very next state (X). This directly translates "At the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start" within the context of PCV Mode.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the system is in PCV Mode and the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) while the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause) must immediately start. The implication ensures that the condition (inspiratoryPhaseEnd AND inspiratoryPauseButton) acts as a trigger for the inspiratoryPause to become true within the PCV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode) and the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) while the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then in the next state (X), an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause) shall start. This accurately captures the condition and the resulting action as described in the natural language.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV Mode, then whenever the inspiratory phase ends AND the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start in the very next state. The `G` operator ensures this holds continuously. The `PCVMode ->` acts as a guard, ensuring the condition is only evaluated when in PCV mode. The `inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton` forms the combined trigger condition. Finally, `X inspiratoryPause` indicates that the inspiratory pause must start in the immediate next state following the trigger.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in PCV Mode, then whenever an inspiratory phase ends AND the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (indicated by 'inspiratoryPauseButton'), an Inspiratory Pause ('inspiratoryPause') must start in the very next state. This accurately captures the requirement that the pause shall start right after the conditions are met.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode), and the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and the Inspiratory Pause is set via the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then an Inspiratory Pause shall start in the very next state (X inspiratoryPause). This captures the conditional trigger of the inspiratory pause at the end of an inspiration phase when specified by the GUI in PCV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode, then at any point where the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) and the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause) must start in the very next state (X). This captures the requirement that the inspiratory pause 'shall start' immediately following the specified conditions.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode), and it is the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause) shall start. The phrase "shall start" implies that the inspiratory pause state becomes active in the same state where these conditions are met.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV Mode, and the inspiratory phase ends, and the Inspiratory Pause has been set by the GUI, then an Inspiratory Pause must begin in the very next state. The 'PCVMode' ensures the property only applies to this specific mode. The 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' acts as the trigger event. The 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is the condition set by the GUI. The 'X inspiratoryPause' ensures that the inspiratory pause starts immediately in the subsequent state after the conditions are met, capturing the 'shall start' requirement.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode) and it is the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then in the next state (X), an Inspiratory Pause shall start (inspiratoryPause). The 'shall start' is captured by the 'X' (next) operator, implying immediate initiation in the subsequent state.
